title: `links deploy` update — hosted deploy via API key
description: Update `links deploy` (without --self) to deploy to the hosted kntic.link platform using the API key from .links.secret. The --self path is unchanged. Add --api flag to both paths for consistency.

features: Add --api <url> option to the deploy command: base URL of the kntic.link API. Default: 'https://api.kntic.link'. Applies only to hosted deploy (not --self).
Look for .links.secret in the same directory as links.yaml. Read the API key (trim whitespace).
If .links.secret is missing or empty: print 'No API key found. Run: links register' and exit 1.
Generate the HTML page using generatePage() (same as --self) — the backend stores the pre-rendered HTML.
POST to <api>/deploy with: Authorization header 'Bearer <api_key>', Content-Type 'application/json', body: { html: <generated HTML string>, config: <config object> }.
On HTTP 200/201: parse response, expect { page_url: string, updated_at: string }. Print: '✓ Deployed to <page_url>'
On HTTP 401: print 'Invalid or expired API key. Run: links register --force' and exit 1.
On HTTP 4xx/5xx: print error from response body if available, exit 1.
On network error: print clear error and exit 1.
Remove the existing stub message ('Other deploy targets are not yet implemented') — hosted deploy is now implemented.
--self path: unchanged in behaviour. --api flag is accepted but ignored when --self is used (no warning needed).

Add a src/secret.js helper module with two exports: readSecret(configDir) — reads and returns trimmed content of .links.secret or null if not found. secretPath(configDir) — returns the absolute path to .links.secret.

notes: Extract secret reading into src/secret.js so both register.js and deploy.js share the same logic — don't duplicate. Use Node built-in fetch for the deploy POST. The HTML string can be large (up to ~50KB inlined CSS) — that's fine for a JSON POST. config object in the POST body allows the backend to store the canonical config server-side for future re-renders.
